title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 9.280 - Mormon War Remembrance Day designated for July 2
text:
9.280.  Mormon War Remembrance Day designated for July 2. — July second of each year shall be known and designated as "Mormon War Remembrance Day" in honor and recognition of the ten thousand members of the Mormon church who were subjected to injustice and undue suffering through Executive Order 44 by Governor Lilburn Boggs and the Mormon War in 1838.
(L. 2022 H.B. 1738)
